cases.title: Testing-plan session open/close row: post-close check-in rejected with SessionClosed
preconditions: Class session exists and was transitioned Scheduled to Open by assigned Lecturer per testing-plan §3.1 Session open/close coverage block.
At least one student is actively enrolled in the session class section.
Session is subsequently closed via lecturer close or policy auto-close path.
steps: Transition session to Closed and record closedAt timestamp.
Submit POST /v1/check-ins as enrolled student with qrToken bound to the closed session.
Query CheckInAttempt and AttendanceRecord rows for the student-session pair.
Verify no new Present or Late success row is created.
cases.expected: Check-in is rejected with SessionClosed outcome; failed CheckInAttempt is persisted with structured reason; no new successful AttendanceRecord is created per AC-05 and BR-02.

cases.title: Check-in service persists failed CheckInAttempt with SessionClosed after session closure
preconditions: Integration harness uses real DB transaction boundaries for class_sessions, check_in_attempts, and attendance_records tables.
Open session row exists with closedAt null; enrolled student has no success attendance yet.
Close command transitions session to Closed within same test transaction scope.
steps: Execute session close through application service boundary.
Invoke check-in command (POST /v1/check-ins path) against the Closed session with valid token binding.
Query check_in_attempts table for student and session.
Query attendance_records table for the same student-session pair.
Assert session row state remains Closed with closedAt unchanged.
cases.expected: Exactly one CheckInAttempt row is inserted with outcome SessionClosed and submittedAt after closedAt; no AttendanceRecord success row is created; session state remains Closed.

cases.title: Closed session state unchanged after rejected post-close check-in attempt
preconditions: Session fixture in Open state with known sessionId and enrolled students.
Lecturer or policy scheduler can invoke Open to Closed transition.
BR-13 absent finalization may run as close side effect.
steps: Capture beforeState as Closed after invoking close transition.
Invoke check-in command for enrolled student without prior success record.
Capture afterState and rejection code SessionClosed.
Query persistence and confirm session state and closedAt are unchanged.
Verify unresolved students receive Absent per BR-13 close side effect independently of rejected check-in.
cases.expected: beforeState equals afterState (Closed); check-in returns SessionClosed; no illegal session state mutation; close finalization side effects remain consistent.

cases.title: POST /v1/check-ins on Closed session returns 422 SessionClosed envelope
preconditions: Class session was Open and is now Closed with closedAt recorded.
Enrolled Student is authenticated with Bearer token and Idempotency-Key header.
qrToken is non-empty and bound to the closed session.
steps: Send POST /v1/check-ins with qrToken, Authorization, and Idempotency-Key headers.
Validate HTTP status code and JSON envelope shape {data, meta, error}.
Assert data is null and error.code is SessionClosed.
Assert error.message is localized Vietnamese text instructing student that attendance has ended and to contact lecturer for manual fallback.
Assert meta includes requestId and timestamp fields.
Query GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance and confirm no new success row for this attempt.
cases.expected: HTTP 422 rule-failure response with standard envelope; error.code SessionClosed; localized Vietnamese message per API design §2.3 and BR-02 outcome; no successful attendance outcome returned.

cases.title: Rule-order short-circuit: SessionClosed returned before token, enrollment, and duplicate checks
preconditions: Closed session exists with closedAt recorded.
Student is not enrolled OR has prior successful attendance OR token is expired—conditions that would fail BR-06, BR-07, or BR-03 if session were Open.
Student actor is authenticated.
steps: Submit POST /v1/check-ins with expired qrToken against Closed session.
Assert error.code is SessionClosed, not ExpiredQr.
Submit with unenrolled student token against Closed session.
Assert error.code is SessionClosed, not NotEnrolled.
Submit duplicate-prone payload for student with existing success record against Closed session.
Assert error.code is SessionClosed, not DuplicateCheckIn.
cases.expected: Validation order per API design §5.1 and validation rules §2.2: session state check (BR-02) short-circuits before BR-03, BR-06, and BR-07 evaluations when session is Closed.

cases.title: Post-close duplicate submission resolves to SessionClosed not DuplicateCheckIn
preconditions: Student already has one successful check-in while session was Open.
Session is then closed by assigned Lecturer.
Student attempts another check-in after closure.
steps: Student re-submits POST /v1/check-ins after session is Closed.
Capture returned HTTP status, error.code, and error.message.
Verify successful attendance row count for student-session remains exactly one.
Query CheckInAttempt rows and confirm latest attempt has SessionClosed outcome.
cases.expected: API returns SessionClosed for post-close attempts even when student has prior success; BR-07 DuplicateCheckIn is not evaluated because BR-02 short-circuits first; no additional successful attendance record is created.

cases.title: Non-lecturer roles denied session close; Student cannot bypass Closed gate via close
preconditions: One Open session exists in lecturer-owned section.
Student and ITAdmin users are authenticated.
Permission matrix: only assigned Lecturer or AcademicAdmin may close sessions.
steps: Student calls POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/close.
Assert HTTP 403 Forbidden or OutOfScope.
ITAdmin calls POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/close without academic grant.
Assert HTTP 403 Forbidden or OutOfScope.
Assigned Lecturer closes session as positive control.
Student submits POST /v1/check-ins and receives SessionClosed.
cases.expected: Unauthorized close attempts denied per BR-19 without altering session state; only authorized close transitions session to Closed; subsequent student check-in is rejected with SessionClosed.

cases.title: Student export denied and cross-scope Lecturer cannot access closed session roster
preconditions: Closed session exists in section A assigned to Lecturer-A.
Lecturer-B is authenticated but not assigned to section A.
Student account is authenticated.
BR-18 scopes export to actor role; BR-19 denies unauthorized report access.
steps: Student calls POST /v1/exports/attendance with filters targeting section A closed session.
Assert HTTP 403 Forbidden without partial export data leakage.
Lecturer-B calls GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance for section A closed session.
Assert HTTP 403 OutOfScope or Forbidden.
Lecturer-A calls the same attendance endpoint as scoped positive control and receives roster with Closed state.
cases.expected: Student institution-wide export denied per BR-18; cross-scope Lecturer denied roster read per BR-19 without data leakage; assigned Lecturer succeeds and sees Closed session state.

cases.title: Idempotent replay on Closed session returns consistent SessionClosed not DuplicateCheckIn
preconditions: Closed session exists with enrolled student.
Test harness can replay POST /v1/check-ins with identical Idempotency-Key, actor, and token.
Student has no successful AttendanceRecord for this session.
steps: Submit first POST /v1/check-ins against Closed session; record SessionClosed outcome.
Replay identical request with same Idempotency-Key immediately.
Submit parallel duplicate requests with same key from harness.
Query check_in_attempts and attendance_records for student-session.
Confirm no Present/Late success row exists.
cases.expected: All replays return deterministic SessionClosed per idempotency rules §7.1; BR-07 DuplicateCheckIn is not evaluated because BR-02 short-circuits first; no success attendance record created under retry storm.

cases.title: In-flight check-in at close boundary: one success per session under overlap
preconditions: Open session with enrolled student; harness can issue parallel POST /v1/check-ins and POST close.
Student has no prior successful AttendanceRecord.
Idempotency-Key headers are unique per distinct command but replayable for same command.
steps: Launch parallel student check-in submission and lecturer close command against same session.
Collect outcomes for check-in attempts and session final state.
Replay successful check-in Idempotency-Key after session is Closed.
Query attendance_records and confirm at most one success row per student-session.
Verify any post-close attempts return SessionClosed.
cases.expected: Under close/check-in race, at most one successful attendance record per student-session (BR-07); replays after closure return SessionClosed; idempotency preserves deterministic outcomes per NFR-07.

cases.title: Alternate path: policy auto-close blocks late student check-in after window ends
preconditions: Open session with effective policy auto-close offset configured.
Enrolled student has not checked in before auto-close trigger.
System scheduler or policy evaluation can transition Open to Closed per BR-21.
steps: Allow session to auto-close when current time exceeds scheduled start plus late window end.
Verify session state is Closed and active QR tokens are invalidated.
Student submits POST /v1/check-ins with previously scanned token.
Observe SessionClosed rejection and absent finalization for unresolved student per BR-13.
Confirm lecturer manual correction path remains available within edit window per BR-02 exception.
cases.expected: Auto-close branch transitions session to Closed; subsequent student self check-in rejected with SessionClosed; unresolved students assigned Absent; manual fallback via BR-14/BR-16 remains available.

cases.title: Student mobile UI shows Vietnamese SessionClosed error and lecturer-contact guidance after session closes
preconditions: Lecturer UI can open and close a session for an assigned section.
Student is logged in on mobile browser profile (iOS Safari or Android Chrome).
Student navigates to check-in page via QR scan or deep link with token preserved.
steps: Lecturer opens session and confirms Open badge/indicator is visible on lecturer session view.
Lecturer closes the same session and verifies session status badge changes to Closed.
Student attempts to submit check-in from check-in form after closure.
Observe error banner or inline message in Vietnamese explaining attendance has ended.
Verify message instructs student to contact lecturer for manual fallback if check-in was legitimate.
Confirm success confirmation UI is not shown and no Present/Late badge appears for the student.
cases.expected: Browser journey surfaces BR-02 rejection with localized Vietnamese SessionClosed copy and lecturer-contact guidance; no false success state; Closed badge visible on lecturer view.
